perturbation.original_text: Article IV. Service Fee The fees for services mentioned in Part I to Part VI are NTD 12 million ((SAY TWELVE MILLION ONLY) in total and the fees for planning and design of the buildings and construction monitoring shall be calculated based on the ratio of 3.50% of the legal construction cost hereof. Should Party A require a comprehensive modification on the development plan of the project outsourced and any subsequent extra cost occur in Party B; Party A shall bear the corresponding extra service fee.
perturbation.changed_text: Article IV. Service Fee The fees for services mentioned in Part I to Part VI are NTD 12 million ((SAY TWELVE MILLION ONLY) in total. The fees for planning and design of the buildings and construction monitoring are subject to further negotiation, and shall not exceed 1% of the legal construction cost. Should Party A require a comprehensive modification on the development plan of the project outsourced and any subsequent extra cost occur in Party B; the possibility of additional service fees will be evaluated on a case-by-case basis and are not guaranteed.
perturbation.explanation: The original contract states a fixed percentage (3.50%) for the fees associated with planning, design, and construction monitoring. The modified text introduces uncertainty by stating these fees are "subject to further negotiation, and shall not exceed 1%", contradicting the fixed percentage. Additionally, it changes that subsequent extra costs "will be evaluated on a case-by-case basis and are not guaranteed", which it previously guaranteed said service fee. This creates contradiction and unclarity about the calculation of these fees.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article IV. Service Fee The fees for services mentioned in Part I to Part VI are NTD 12 million ((SAY TWELVE MILLION ONLY) in total. The fees for planning and design of the buildings and construction monitoring are subject to further negotiation, and shall not exceed 1% of the legal construction cost. Should Party A require a comprehensive modification on the development plan of the project outsourced and any subsequent extra cost occur in Party B; the possibility of additional service fees will be evaluated on a case-by-case basis and are not guaranteed.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Article IV introduces a contradiction by changing the fee calculation method from a fixed percentage (3.50% of the legal construction cost) to a negotiable amount not exceeding 1%. This directly conflicts with the original agreement of a fixed rate. Furthermore, it alters the guarantee of extra service fees for comprehensive modifications to an evaluation on a case-by-case basis with no guarantee, which is also found within the same Article IV. This creates uncertainty and contradicts the original terms.

perturbation.original_text: In case the Contract cannot be performed due to any reason other than the architect herein, the client agrees the fees paid will not be refunded. In case the Contract cannot be performed due to the architect herein, the architect shall refund the fees paid in full without taking any interest to the client.
perturbation.changed_text: In case the Contract cannot be performed due to any reason other than the architect herein, the client may be entitled to a partial refund of the fees paid, subject to the discretion of Party B. In case the Contract cannot be performed due to the architect herein, the architect may not be required to refund the fees paid, and this decision will be subject to further negotiation.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly defines the refund policy. If the contract is terminated due to reasons other than the architect's fault, no refund is issued. If the contract is terminated due to the architect's fault, a full refund is issued. The modified text introduces uncertainty. It states that the client "may be entitled to a partial refund" (rather than no refund) and that the architect "may not be required to refund the fees paid" (rather than a full refund), making both scenarios subject to discretion and further negotiation, contradicting the initial fixed clauses. This can lead to disputes as it is not clear under what circumstances and how much refund is to be issued.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Stage 10: The final service fee shall be paid in a lump sum upon the completion of the structure of relevant buildings and submitting the usage license application to the competent authority. In case the Contract cannot be performed due to any reason other than the architect herein, the client may be entitled to a partial refund of the fees paid, subject to the discretion of Party B. In case the Contract cannot be performed due to the architect herein, the architect may not be required to refund the fees paid, and this decision will be subject to further negotiation.
perturbation.justification: The original text established a clear refund policy: no refund if termination isn't the architect's fault, full refund if it is. The changed text introduces ambiguity and subjectivity by stating the client 'may be entitled to a partial refund' and the architect 'may not be required to refund', making both contingent on discretion and negotiation. This directly contradicts the original fixed obligation. The contradicted text highlights that 'In case the Contract cannot be performed due to any reason other than the architect herein, the client may be entitled to a partial refund of the fees paid, subject to the discretion of Party B. In case the Contract cannot be performed due to the architect herein, the architect may not be required to refund the fees paid, and this decision will be subject to further negotiation.' This directly conflicts with the implicit understanding of no refund or full refund, causing a contradiction and aligns with the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition where key terms are defined inconsistently, making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: Article IX. Duties of Party B I. Party B shall follow all instructions of Party A and ensure all plans and designs of the project to meet relevant construction laws and regulations. II. Party B shall take on a professional attitude for various services mentioned in Article III of the Contract, maintain benefits and interests of Party A all the time and adopt the most economical option under the precondition of safety and reliability. III. Party B is obligatory to report the latest progress and completed content to Party A.
perturbation.changed_text: Article IX. Duties of Party B I. Party B shall follow all reasonable instructions of Party A and ensure all plans and designs of the project to meet relevant construction laws and regulations. II. Party B shall strive to take on a professional attitude for various services mentioned in Article III of the Contract, but cannot guarantee the benefits and interests of Party A at all times. The adoption of the most economical option is subject to Party B's internal approval. III. Party B is encouraged to report the latest progress and completed content to Party A, as time permits.
perturbation.explanation: The original contract dictates that Party B MUST follow all instructions, maintain benefits and interests of Party A all the time, and adopt the most economical option, as well as report the project's progress. The modified text introduces uncertainty by stating Party B must only follow 'all reasonable instructions', implying that Party B can determine what is reasonable and what is not. In addition, it says Party B can only 'strive' to maintain benefits and interests and that adoption of the economical option is subject to Party B's 'internal approval', and finally, reporting the project's progress is only encouraged 'as time permits'. All these edits contradict the definitive instructions mentioned in the original text, and creates potential disputes around the actual duties and liabilities of Party B.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article IX. Duties of Party B I. Party B shall follow all reasonable instructions of Party A and ensure all plans and designs of the project to meet relevant construction laws and regulations. II. Party B shall strive to take on a professional attitude for various services mentioned in Article III of the Contract, but cannot guarantee the benefits and interests of Party A at all times. The adoption of the most economical option is subject to Party B's internal approval. III. Party B is encouraged to report the latest progress and completed content to Party A, as time permits.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens Party B's obligations compared to the original text, creating a contradiction. Specifically, the original text states Party B 'shall follow all instructions,' while the changed text says 'shall follow all reasonable instructions,' implying Party B has discretion. Further, 'maintain benefits and interests of Party A all the time' is weakened to 'strive to take on a professional attitude, but cannot guarantee the benefits and interests of Party A at all times.' The introduction of 'internal approval' for the 'most economical option' and changing 'obligatory to report' to 'encouraged to report, as time permits' all contradict the initial definitive tone and create ambiguity. All these edits contradict the definitive instructions mentioned in the original text, and creates potential disputes around the actual duties and liabilities of Party B.
